edges.description: Functional lysosomal hydrolase deficiency leads to multisystem substrate accumulation.
edges.source: GlcNAc-1-Phosphotransferase Deficiency and Mannose-6-Phosphate Targeting Failure

edges.target: GlcNAc-1-Phosphotransferase Deficiency and Mannose-6-Phosphate Targeting Failure

evidence.reference_title: The lysosomal storage disorders mucolipidosis type II, type III alpha/beta, and type III gamma: Update on GNPTAB and GNPTG mutations.
evidence.snippet: Mutations in the GNPTAB and GNPTG genes cause mucolipidosis (ML) type II

nodes.description: Biallelic GNPTAB variants abolish GlcNAc-1-phosphotransferase, preventing formation of the mannose-6-phosphate marker that targets soluble acid hydrolases to the lysosome. Untagged enzymes are missorted and hypersecreted rather than delivered, leaving the lysosome functionally deficient in multiple hydrolases.
nodes.id: GlcNAc-1-Phosphotransferase Deficiency and Mannose-6-Phosphate Targeting Failure

evidence.reference_title: The lysosomal storage disorders mucolipidosis type II, type III alpha/beta, and type III gamma: Update on GNPTAB and GNPTG mutations.
evidence.snippet: Mutations in the GNPTAB and GNPTG genes cause mucolipidosis (ML) type II

nodes.description: Functional deficiency of multiple lysosomal hydrolases causes accumulation of undegraded substrates across many tissues (skeleton, connective tissue, heart, brain), producing the severe multisystem disease of I-cell disease.

evidence.reference_title: Mucolipidosis type II and type III: a systematic review of 843 published cases.
evidence.snippet:
Mucolipidosis (ML) II, MLIII alpha/beta, and MLIII gamma are rare
autosomal recessive lysosomal storage disorders.
